title: [PAPER] Long-lived remote ion-ion entanglement for scalable quantum repeaters
why:
- The study demonstrates memory-memory entanglement between two nodes connected by 10 km of spooled fibre, with entanglement surviving beyond the average establishment time.
- This achievement is enabled by long-lived trapped-ion memories, an efficient telecom interface, and a high-visibility single-photon entanglement protocol.
- A proof-of-principle device-independent quantum key distribution (DI-QKD) demonstration was achieved over 10 km with finite-size analysis, and a positive key rate was reported over 101 km in the asymptotic limit—both distances exceeding previous work by more than two orders of magnitude.
- The results provide a critical building block for quantum repeaters and mark an important step toward scalable quantum networks.
